nodes.label: Track A — preservation: make the tunnel harmless
nodes.rationale: If early class-relevant features survive to the top via a near-identity residual, 'read the top' == 'read the extractor' and the readout-placement problem dissolves; the author's Sol-2 (residual), named and proven, but with a sharp warning it is not a free win.

nodes.label: ReZero / Fixup near-identity residual gate
nodes.rationale: y=x+alpha*f(x), alpha learnable scalar init to 0 — the block starts as identity, only contributes when it helps; trains thousands of layers; the author's exact idea, named and proven. POST-REVIEW: alpha defaults FROZEN/init-based (Fixup-style), not local-loss-trained, because a locally-trained alpha opens the gate exactly when the block starts drifting off-manifold.

nodes.label: DenseNet / MSDNet dense feature reuse
nodes.rationale: Preserve by concatenation instead of addition; MSDNet combines dense preservation WITH early-exit classifiers at multiple depths — the bridge to Track B; additive (ReZero) is the better substrate fit since concatenation grows width every layer.

nodes.label: Residuals are NOT a free win (bypass-cheat warning)
nodes.rationale: A residual skip can let information route around the layer so it never has to build a good representation — a lazy-bypass cheat path that weakens learned features in SSL/generative settings; the Tunnel Effect's own verdict: preservation extends the extractor, doesn't abolish the tunnel.
